mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets compress wage growth and signal peak economic activity, triggering profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending, which reduces forward revenue expectations for cyclical equities and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, an increase in the industrial production index reflects rebuilding capacity and rising corporate throughput, which directly supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns during identified recovery episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential rise in real GDP growth accelerates aggregate demand, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show positive growth coinciding with negative 5-day equity returns, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5%, the Federal Reserve signals a tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward equity returns remain positive or statistically neutral after the rate crosses the 0.5% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicate an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, which ultimately suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, a sustained decline in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, which erode corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines coincide with positive equity returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.
